query: Diosdado Cabello Tareck El Aissami status May 2026 Venezuela arrest extradition
answer:
Based on the extensive research gathered, here is my comprehensive synthesis.

---

# Diosdado Cabello & Tareck El Aissami — Status as of May 2026

## 1. Diosdado Cabello Rondón

### Current Government Position
Diosdado Cabello has served as **Minister of Interior, Justice and Peace** since **August 27, 2024**, appointed by Nicolás Maduro. He also continues as **Secretary-General of the United Socialist Party of Venezuela (PSUV)**, a role held since December 2011. His daughter, Daniella Cabello, was appointed Venezuelan tourism minister in early 2026, suggesting continued family influence in the post-Maduro-capture government.

### U.S. DOJ Indictment
Cabello is a **named co-defendant** in *United States v. Nicolás Maduro Moros et al.*, Case No. 1:11-cr-00205-AKH, in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York (SDNY).

- **Original indictment** (unsealed March 26, 2020): Charged with **narco-terrorism conspiracy, conspiracy to import cocaine, and weapons charges**, alongside Maduro and 14 other Venezuelan officials.
- **Superseding indictment** (unsealed January 3, 2026): Again named in the expanded indictment that added Cilia Flores, Nicolás Maduro Guerra, and Tren de Aragua leader Niño Guerrero.

### U.S. Reward
- **Original reward** (March 2020): **$10 million** for information leading to arrest or conviction.
- **Increased reward** (January 2025): The DEA announced a new **$25 million reward** for Cabello's capture, coinciding with Maduro's disputed third-term inauguration.

### OFAC Sanctions
Cabello has been **sanctioned by OFAC since May 18, 2018**. He is also sanctioned by **Canada** (Sept 2017), the **European Union** (Jan 2018), **Switzerland** (March 2018), **Mexico** (April 2018), and **Panama** (March 2018), and is banned from entering Colombia. Approximately **$800 million in assets** reportedly frozen.

### Arrest & Extradition Status
As of May 2026, Cabello is **at large in Venezuela**. He has **not been arrested** and **no extradition proceedings** have been initiated. Venezuela does not have an extradition treaty with the United States and would not comply with any such request. However, in **September 2024**, a federal court in **Buenos Aires, Argentina** issued an **arrest warrant** against Cabello for **crimes against humanity**.

### Post-Maduro-Capture Context
Following Maduro's capture by U.S. special forces on January 3, 2026 (Operation Absolute Resolve), Cabello remains in his ministerial role under acting president **Delcy Rodríguez**. His continued governmental position and family appointments suggest he retains significant power in the post-capture government structure.

---

## 2. Tareck El Aissami

### Current Status
Tareck El Aissami is **imprisoned in Venezuela** at **Fuerte Tiuna**, a military installation in Caracas. He holds **no current government position**. His last role was Minister of Petroleum (April 2020 – March 20, 2023).

### Timeline of Downfall
1. **March 20, 2023**: El Aissami **resigned** from his position as Minister of Petroleum via Twitter amid a widening **anti-corruption probe** targeting PDVSA (state oil company). Replaced by Pedro Rafael Tellechea.
2. **April 9–10, 2024**: El Aissami was **arrested by the Venezuelan prosecutor's office** on charges of **treason, money laundering, criminal association, and embezzlement**.

### U.S. DOJ & Sanctions
- **February 13, 2017**: Sanctioned by **OFAC** under the **Foreign Narcotics Kingpin Designation Act** while serving as Vice President of Venezuela. Accused of facilitating drug shipments from Venezuela to Mexico and the U.S.
- **March 26, 2020**: U.S. State Department offered a **$10 million reward** for information leading to his arrest or conviction.
- **2019**: Added to the **ICE Most Wanted List** by Homeland Security Investigations.
- He is also sanctioned by **Canada** (Sept 2017), the **EU** (June 2018), and **Switzerland** (July 2018).

### Extradition Status
There are **no known extradition proceedings** as of May 2026. His situation is unusual: he is already imprisoned domestically on Venezuelan charges, while simultaneously remaining wanted by the United States on separate narco-terrorism and drug trafficking charges. The post-Maduro Venezuelan government under Delcy Rodríguez has shown no public movement toward extraditing him.

### Analytical Note
El Aissami's domestic prosecution has been characterized by analysts as potentially a **political maneuver** rather than genuine anti-corruption enforcement, given that the same Maduro government that elevated him also accused him of corruption in what appeared to be an internal power consolidation.

---

## Summary Comparison

| Attribute | Diosdado Cabello | Tareck El Aissami |
|---|---|---|
| **Current Position** | Minister of Interior; PSUV Secretary-General | None — stripped of all positions |
| **Physical Status** | At large in Venezuela | Imprisoned at Fuerte Tiuna, Caracas |
| **U.S. Indictment** | Named co-defendant, SDNY narco-terrorism case (2020, expanded Jan 2026) | OFAC kingpin designation; $10M reward; ICE Most Wanted |
| **U.S. Reward** | $25 million (increased Jan 2025) | $10 million (since March 2020) |
| **OFAC Sanctions** | Yes (since May 2018) | Yes (since February 2017) |
| **Domestic Arrest** | No | Yes (April 2024) — treason, money laundering, embezzlement |
| **U.S. Extradition** | None initiated | None initiated |
| **Argentine Warrant** | Yes (Sept 2024) — crimes against humanity | Not reported |

---

key_facts: Diosdado Cabello serves as Venezuela's Minister of Interior, Justice and Peace (since Aug 27, 2024) and PSUV Secretary-General; he remains at large in Venezuela as of May 2026.

Cabello is a named co-defendant in SDNY Case No. 1:11-cr-00205-AKH (U.S. v. Maduro Moros et al.), charged with narco-terrorism conspiracy, conspiracy to import cocaine, and weapons charges (original indictment March 2020; superseding indictment January 3, 2026).
The U.S. reward for Cabello was increased from $10 million to $25 million in January 2025.
Cabello has been sanctioned by OFAC since May 18, 2018, with approximately $800 million in assets reportedly frozen; also sanctioned by Canada, EU, Switzerland, Mexico, and Panama.
An Argentine federal court issued an arrest warrant for Cabello in September 2024 for crimes against humanity.
No extradition proceedings have been initiated for Cabello; Venezuela has no extradition treaty with the U.S.

Tareck El Aissami is imprisoned at Fuerte Tiuna military installation in Caracas, Venezuela, following his April 2024 arrest on charges of treason, money laundering, criminal association, and embezzlement.
El Aissami resigned as Minister of Petroleum on March 20, 2023, amid a widening PDVSA anti-corruption probe.
El Aissami was sanctioned by OFAC under the Foreign Narcotics Kingpin Designation Act in February 2017 while serving as Vice President; a $10 million U.S. reward remains outstanding.
No U.S. extradition proceedings have been initiated for El Aissami despite his imprisonment in Venezuela; the post-Maduro-capture government under Delcy Rodríguez has not moved to extradite him.

Following Maduro's capture by U.S. forces on January 3, 2026 (Operation Absolute Resolve), Cabello retained his ministerial role under acting president Delcy Rodríguez, while El Aissami remains imprisoned.
El Aissami's domestic prosecution is viewed by some analysts as a political maneuver/internal power consolidation rather than genuine anti-corruption enforcement.
